records.ety: [F. maladie, fr. malade ill, sick, OF. also, malabde, fr. L. male habitus, i. e., ill-kept, not in good condition. See Malice, and Habit.]

defs: Any disease of the human body; a distemper, disorder, or indisposition, proceeding from impaired, defective, or morbid organic functions; especially, a lingering or deep-seated disorder.
quotes: The maladies of the body may prove medicines to the mind.

defs: A moral or mental defect or disorder.
quotes: Love's a malady without a cure.
